binaries.title: csvfs.sys (Cluster Shared Volume File System) - TOCTOU Race Condition / Use-After-Free in CsvCamSetSecurityInfo EncryptionManager update

changed_functions.name: CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C00BCAAC)
key_changes: UNPATCHED: reads the global Encryption* pointer EncryptionManager (?EncryptionManager@@3PEAVEncryption@@EA) WITHOUT holding SigningLock when *(arg1+0xe8) != 0, then dereferences it (*(rdx+0x20) = stored size, *(rdx+0x18) = stored buffer) and passes those to memcmp (sub_1C0018950). A concurrent thread in the exclusive-write path can free this Encryption object via Encryption::`scalar deleting destructor' (??_GEncryption, sub_1C0058100 -> ExFreePoolWithTag), causing Use-After-Free

PATCHED: wraps the read+dereference of EncryptionManager in a shared lock via RWLock::AcquireShared (?AcquireShared@RWLock@@QEAAXXZ, sub_1C0018818 in the patched build) / RWLock::ReleaseShared (?ReleaseShared@RWLock@@QEAAXXZ, sub_1C0018850 in the patched build). This is the shared side of SigningLock, the same RWLock whose exclusive side guards the free, so the read now serializes against the exclusive writer

INCIDENTAL (not security-relevant): UNPATCHED uses 'test eax, eax; jz' and PATCHED uses 'cmp [rcx+0xe8], ebp; jbe'. For a length field (never negative) these tests are equivalent; the jbe form does not reject any additional value

INCIDENTAL (not security-relevant): UNPATCHED caches *(arg1+0xe8) in eax at function entry (0x1C00BCAC4) and reuses it; PATCHED drops the cache and re-reads *(arg1+0xe8) from memory after acquiring the lock (cmp [rsi+0xe8], eax at 0x1C00BCAF2). This is a codegen consequence of the source change, not an independent fix

The second global object ReplayCacheManagerObject (read at 0x1C00BCC52 unpatched / 0x1C00BCC45 patched) is already read under SigningLock shared in BOTH builds; only the EncryptionManager read was missing lock protection in the unpatched build

The two shared-lock helpers RWLock::AcquireShared (0x1C0018818) and RWLock::ReleaseShared (0x1C0018850) exist ONLY in the patched build (0 occurrences in the unpatched build). Their bodies hardcode the global SigningLock (AcquireShared: KeEnterCriticalRegion + ExAcquireResourceSharedLite([SigningLock],1); ReleaseShared: ExReleaseResourceLite([SigningLock]) + KeLeaveCriticalRegion), so the caller's rcx is irrelevant. In the unpatched build this exact sequence was emitted inline for the ReplayCacheManagerObject read; the patch factors it into these helpers and newly applies it to the EncryptionManager read. At 0x1C0018818 the unpatched build instead contains __GSHandlerCheck (relocated to 0x1C0018888 in the patched build)
changed_functions.security_notes: TOCTOU race condition leading to Use-After-Free (CWE-416/CWE-362) in CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C00BCAAC at 0x1C00BCAAC in both builds). The function updates the global Encryption signing manager. It reads the global Encryption* EncryptionManager (a 0x28-byte object allocated with ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool, 0x28, 'CVCA'=0x41435643)) and dereferences its stored-buffer field (+0x18) and stored-size field (+0x20), comparing the caller's input buffer (arg1+0xec, length arg1+0xe8) against the stored buffer via memcmp (sub_1C0018950). In the UNPATCHED build this read/deref happens WITHOUT holding SigningLock. Later in the same function the exclusive-write path takes KeEnterCriticalRegion + ExAcquireResourceExclusiveLite on SigningLock (?SigningLock@@3PEAVRWLock@@EA), frees the old EncryptionManager via Encryption::`scalar deleting destructor' (sub_1C0058100 -> ExFreePoolWithTag), and replaces the global with a freshly allocated object. If two threads run this path concurrently, Thread A can read the global pointer, Thread B can free it and replace it, and Thread A then dereferences freed memory. The PATCHED build wraps the read in RWLock::AcquireShared / RWLock::ReleaseShared (the shared side of SigningLock), blocking the exclusive writer from freeing the object during the read. Reachable from CsvFsHandleMessage (sub_1C0005F50) -> CsvFsHandleSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0046974) -> CsvCamSetSecurityInfo.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (vulnerable - NO lock around the EncryptionManager read):
0x1C00BCAC4: mov     eax, [rcx+0xe8]              ; cache input length in eax
0x1C00BCAD5: test    eax, eax                    ; != 0
0x1C00BCAD7: jz      0x1C00BCC2E
0x1C00BCADD: mov     rdx, cs:EncryptionManager   ; NO LOCK HELD
0x1C00BCAE7: test    rdx, rdx
0x1C00BCAEA: jz      0x1C00BCB63
0x1C00BCAEC: mov     ecx, [rdx+0x20]             ; deref stored size (unlocked)
0x1C00BCAEF: cmp     eax, ecx
0x1C00BCAF3: mov     rdx, [rdx+0x18]             ; deref stored buffer (unlocked)
0x1C00BCB01: call    memcmp                      ; sub_1C0018950
; exclusive path later in same function:
0x1C00BCBD0: mov     rbx, cs:SigningLock
0x1C00BCBD7: call    KeEnterCriticalRegion
0x1C00BCBE8: call    ExAcquireResourceExclusiveLite
0x1C00BCC00: call    ??_GEncryption              ; scalar deleting destructor -> ExFreePoolWithTag (frees old EncryptionManager)
0x1C00BCC0C: mov     cs:EncryptionManager, rdi  ; replace global
0x1C00BCC16: call    ExReleaseResourceLite
0x1C00BCC22: call    KeLeaveCriticalRegion

PATCHED (fixed - shared lock protects the read):
0x1C00BCACF: cmp     [rcx+0xe8], ebp            ; unsigned > 0
0x1C00BCAD5: jbe     0x1C00BCC40
0x1C00BCADE: call    ?AcquireShared@RWLock@@QEAAXXZ ; sub_1C0018818 (SigningLock shared acquire)
0x1C00BCAE3: mov     rdx, cs:EncryptionManager  ; read under shared lock
0x1C00BCAEA: test    rdx, rdx
0x1C00BCAEF: mov     eax, [rdx+0x20]            ; safe deref under lock
0x1C00BCAF2: cmp     [rsi+0xe8], eax            ; re-read arg1+0xe8 from memory (no stale eax)
0x1C00BCAFA: mov     rdx, [rdx+0x18]
0x1C00BCB08: call    memcmp                     ; sub_1C00189C0
0x1C00BCB67: call    ?ReleaseShared@RWLock@@QEAAXXZ ; sub_1C0018850 (SigningLock shared release)

security_findings.entry_point: csvfs.sys message/FSCTL dispatch: CsvFsHandleMessage (sub_1C0005F50) routes a set-security-info message to CsvFsHandleSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0046974), which calls CsvCamSetSecurityInfo
call_chain: DriverEntry (0x1C00BF078, sub_1C00BF078) - driver initialization (init path, not the runtime dispatch of this call)
CsvFsHandleMessage (0x1C0005F50, sub_1C0005F50, message dispatcher; calls CsvFsHandleSetSecurityInfo)
CsvFsHandleSetSecurityInfo (0x1C0046974, sub_1C0046974; validates buffers, calls CsvCamSetSecurityInfo at 0x1C00469D6)
security_findings.description: CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C00BCAAC) manages the global Encryption signing manager. It maintains the global Encryption* EncryptionManager (0x28-byte 'CVCA' object) and the global ReplayCacheManagerObject (0x1010-byte object). When *(arg1+0xe8) (the caller-supplied input length) is non-zero, the UNPATCHED build: (1) reads EncryptionManager WITHOUT any lock, (2) dereferences its stored-size (+0x20) and stored-buffer (+0x18) fields, (3) memcmp's the caller input buffer against the stored buffer, then (4) allocates a replacement Encryption object, takes SigningLock exclusive, frees the old object via Encryption::`scalar deleting destructor' (sub_1C0058100 -> ExFreePoolWithTag), and swaps the global. The race window is between the unlocked pointer read (0x1C00BCADD) and the dereferences (0x1C00BCAEC / 0x1C00BCAF3). A concurrent thread executing the exclusive-write path can free the object Thread A just read, leaving Thread A with a dangling pointer. The patch wraps the read+dereference in RWLock::AcquireShared / RWLock::ReleaseShared (the shared side of SigningLock), which blocks the exclusive writer (ExAcquireResourceExclusiveLite on SigningLock) from freeing the object during the read. The ReplayCacheManagerObject read was already under SigningLock shared in both builds.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. csvfs.sys loaded (Windows Failover Cluster node with Cluster Shared Volumes enabled). 2. Attacker issues two concurrent set-security-info operations that route through CsvFsHandleMessage (sub_1C0005F50) -> CsvFsHandleSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0046974) targeting the same CSV volume. 3. Both must have *(arg1+0xe8) > 0 (non-empty encryption input) to enter the EncryptionManager path. 4. The race window is between reading EncryptionManager (0x1C00BCADD) and dereferencing it (0x1C00BCAEC-0x1C00BCB01). 5. Thread B must execute the exclusive-write free path (around 0x1C00BCBD0-0x1C00BCC29) that calls the Encryption scalar deleting destructor (sub_1C0058100 -> ExFreePoolWithTag). 6. Timing must align so Thread A reads the pointer, Thread B frees it, then Thread A dereferences the freed pointer. CsvFsHandleSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0046974) performs input validation before the call.
security_findings.primitive: Use-After-Free on a 0x28-byte PagedPool allocation (tag 'CVCA' = 0x41435643) holding an Encryption object. The directly demonstrable impact is a kernel crash (DoS): if the freed block is unmapped or poisoned, the racing dereference at 0x1C00BCAEC (mov ecx,[rdx+0x20]) faults. If the freed slot is instead reclaimed with controlled data before the dereference, the +0x18 pointer and +0x20 size read from the reclaimed object are attacker-influenced and flow into memcmp (source pointer and length), producing an out-of-bounds read against kernel memory. Note the memcmp result is consumed only as a match/no-match boolean that decides whether the manager is rebuilt (r14); it is not returned to the caller, so this is not by itself an information-disclosure channel. Reclaiming the 0x28 PagedPool slot with controlled contents is not established against these binaries and is out of scope.
security_findings.assembly:
CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C00BCAAC, csvfs_unpatched.sys) - vulnerable function:

0x1C00BCAAC: mov     [rsp+0x8], rbx
0x1C00BCAB1: mov     [rsp+0x10], rbp
0x1C00BCAB6: mov     [rsp+0x18], rsi
0x1C00BCABB: push    rdi
0x1C00BCABC: push    r14
0x1C00BCABE: push    r15
0x1C00BCAC0: sub     rsp, 0x30
0x1C00BCAC4: mov     eax, [rcx+0xe8]              ; cache input length in eax
0x1C00BCACA: xor     ebp, ebp
0x1C00BCACC: xor     r14b, r14b
0x1C00BCACF: mov     rsi, rcx
0x1C00BCAD2: mov     r15b, 0x1
0x1C00BCAD5: test    eax, eax                    ; != 0
0x1C00BCAD7: jz      0x1C00BCC2E
; === RACE WINDOW START - NO LOCK ===
0x1C00BCADD: mov     rdx, cs:EncryptionManager   ; read global ptr UNLOCKED
0x1C00BCAE4: mov     r14b, r15b
0x1C00BCAE7: test    rdx, rdx
0x1C00BCAEA: jz      0x1C00BCB63
0x1C00BCAEC: mov     ecx, [rdx+0x20]             ; deref unlocked ptr (stored size)
0x1C00BCAEF: cmp     eax, ecx
0x1C00BCAF1: jnz     0x1C00BCB3A
0x1C00BCAF3: mov     rdx, [rdx+0x18]             ; deref unlocked ptr (stored buffer)
0x1C00BCAF7: mov     r8d, ecx                    ; Size
0x1C00BCAFA: lea     rcx, [rsi+0xec]             ; Buf1 (caller input)
0x1C00BCB01: call    memcmp                      ; sub_1C0018950
; === exclusive path that frees the object Thread A may be reading ===
0x1C00BCBD0: mov     rbx, cs:SigningLock
0x1C00BCBD7: call    KeEnterCriticalRegion
0x1C00BCBE3: mov     rcx, [rbx]                  ; Resource
0x1C00BCBE6: mov     dl, 0x1
0x1C00BCBE8: call    ExAcquireResourceExclusiveLite
0x1C00BCBF4: mov     rcx, cs:EncryptionManager   ; old object
0x1C00BCBFB: test    rcx, rcx
0x1C00BCC00: call    ??_GEncryption              ; scalar deleting destructor -> ExFreePoolWithTag (FREE)
0x1C00BCC0C: mov     cs:EncryptionManager, rdi   ; replace global with new object
0x1C00BCC16: call    ExReleaseResourceLite
0x1C00BCC22: call    KeLeaveCriticalRegion
; === ReplayCacheManagerObject read - SigningLock shared in BOTH builds ===
0x1C00BCC2E: mov     rbx, cs:SigningLock
0x1C00BCC35: call    KeEnterCriticalRegion
0x1C00BCC41: mov     rcx, [rbx]
0x1C00BCC44: mov     dl, 0x1
0x1C00BCC46: call    ExAcquireResourceSharedLite
0x1C00BCC52: mov     rax, cs:ReplayCacheManagerObject
0x1C00BCCD0: call    ExReleaseResourceLite
0x1C00BCCDC: call    KeLeaveCriticalRegion
; === Encryption scalar deleting destructor sub_1C0058100 ===
0x1C0058109: call    ?ReleaseAll@Encryption@@AEAAXXZ  ; Encryption::ReleaseAll
0x1C0058113: call    ExFreePoolWithTag               ; deallocate the object

breakpoints.why: Function entry - rcx (arg1) holds the volume/security context. Check [rcx+0xe8] (input length), [rcx+0xe0] and [rcx+0xe4] (volume identifiers used to build the ReplayCacheManager). Break when [rcx+0xe8] != 0 to hit the EncryptionManager path.

The unlocked read of the EncryptionManager global. rdx will hold the Encryption object pointer. Start of the race window - if another thread frees this object before the deref at 0x1C00BCAEC, a UAF occurs.
The vulnerable dereference 'mov ecx, [rdx+0x20]' (stored size). rdx holds the potentially-freed pointer. If the pool has been reclaimed, this reads attacker-controlled data.

The free: 'call ??_GEncryption' (Encryption scalar deleting destructor -> ExFreePoolWithTag). rcx holds the object being freed. Break here to confirm the object being freed matches the one read at 0x1C00BCADD by the other thread.

The Encryption scalar deleting destructor - confirms Encryption::ReleaseAll then ExFreePoolWithTag on the cache object. Observe the pool address being freed, then check if any other thread still holds a reference to it.

watch: rcx at function entry (0x1C00BCAAC): arg1 = security context; [rcx+0xe8] must be > 0 to enter the EncryptionManager path
rdx at 0x1C00BCADD: the unlocked EncryptionManager pointer - this is the pointer that can become dangling
[rdx+0x20] / [rdx+0x18] at 0x1C00BCAEC / 0x1C00BCAF3: stored size and stored buffer dereferenced without lock
rcx at 0x1C00BCC00: object passed to ??_GEncryption (free) - if this equals rdx from 0x1C00BCADD on another thread, the UAF is confirmed
EncryptionManager global: the pointer being raced on - watch for it changing between 0x1C00BCADD and 0x1C00BCAEC
Pool tag 'CVCA' (0x41435643): the Encryption object tag; use !poolfind 0x41435643 to enumerate live allocations
key_offsets: 0x1C00BCAC4: 'mov eax, [rcx+0xe8]' - reads and caches the input length (patch removes this cache and re-reads from memory under the lock)
0x1C00BCAD5: 'test eax, eax' - signed != 0 (patch uses 'cmp [rcx+0xe8], ebp; jbe' for unsigned > 0)
0x1C00BCADD: 'mov rdx, cs:EncryptionManager' - UNLOCKED read of the Encryption global (patch adds 'call ?AcquireShared@RWLock' at 0x1C00BCADE before this)
0x1C00BCAEC: 'mov ecx, [rdx+0x20]' - UNLOCKED dereference (stored size), UAF sink
0x1C00BCB01: 'call memcmp' (sub_1C0018950) - compares caller input against the stored buffer
0x1C00BCC00: 'call ??_GEncryption' (sub_1C0058100) - frees the old Encryption object under SigningLock exclusive (the free that races with the read)
0x1C00BCC0C: 'mov cs:EncryptionManager, rdi' - replaces the global after free
0x1C00BCC2E-0x1C00BCC46: ReplayCacheManagerObject read path - already under SigningLock SHARED in both builds (contrast shows the EncryptionManager read was missing it)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Ensure csvfs.sys is loaded (Failover Cluster node with Cluster Shared Volumes configured). 2. Open a handle reaching the CSV set-security-info path. 3. Drive a set-security-info operation that routes through CsvFsHandleMessage (sub_1C0005F50) -> CsvFsHandleSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0046974) with a non-empty encryption input so that the context field at +0xe8 is > 0. 4. Race two or more concurrent operations from multiple threads targeting the same volume to hit the window between the pointer read (0x1C00BCADD) and dereference (0x1C00BCAEC). 5. Use thread affinity/priority to widen the window. Spray 0x28-byte 'CVCA'-class PagedPool objects between the free and the dereference to reclaim the allocation with controlled data.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: BSOD PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA (0x50) or an invalid pool read when Thread A dereferences the freed pointer at 0x1C00BCAEC ('mov ecx, [rdx+0x20]'). The faulting address is a freed PagedPool allocation of size 0x28 with tag 'CVCA'. If the pool is reclaimed with controlled data before the dereference, no crash occurs but the function memcmp's against attacker-controlled stored-buffer/size values. With Driver Verifier Special Pool, the crash occurs at free+reuse with 'Parameter 1 = freed pool address'.
debugging_aids.notes: The 0x28-byte Encryption object (tag 'CVCA') is allocated from PagedPool. Pool spray for this size class can use common user-mode primitives. The race window is between 0x1C00BCADD (pointer read) and 0x1C00BCAEC (pointer dereference), extending through the memcmp at 0x1C00BCB01 which reads from the stored buffer of the (possibly freed) object. The lock is SigningLock (?SigningLock@@3PEAVRWLock@@EA), a C++ RWLock wrapping an ERESOURCE; the exclusive side (ExAcquireResourceExclusiveLite) performs the free, and the patch adds the matching shared side (RWLock::AcquireShared / ReleaseShared) around the read. Address 0x1C0018818 resolves to RWLock::AcquireShared in the patched build but to __GSHandlerCheck in the unpatched build (different functions at the same address across builds); the fix is the added shared-lock acquire/release, not a change to any helper.

summary: The patch fixes a TOCTOU race condition / Use-After-Free (CWE-416/CWE-362) in the Windows Cluster Shared Volume File System driver (csvfs.sys), in CsvCamSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C00BCAAC) at 0x1C00BCAAC (same address in both builds). The unpatched code reads the global Encryption* EncryptionManager (a 0x28-byte 'CVCA'-tagged PagedPool object) and dereferences its stored-size (+0x20) and stored-buffer (+0x18) fields, then memcmp's the caller input against the stored buffer, all WITHOUT holding SigningLock, while a concurrent thread can take SigningLock exclusive, free the same object via Encryption::`scalar deleting destructor' (sub_1C0058100 -> ExFreePoolWithTag), and replace the global. The patch wraps the read+dereference in RWLock::AcquireShared / RWLock::ReleaseShared (sub_1C0018818 / sub_1C0018850 in the patched build - the shared side of SigningLock), preventing the exclusive writer from freeing the object during reads (matching the protection already present on the ReplayCacheManagerObject read). Note: address 0x1C0018818 is __GSHandlerCheck in the unpatched build and RWLock::AcquireShared in the patched build. Reachable via CsvFsHandleMessage (sub_1C0005F50) -> CsvFsHandleSetSecurityInfo (sub_1C0046974) -> CsvCamSetSecurityInfo.
